examples.title: Pseudocode: IAM Access Control Flow
examples.description: Demonstrates the IAM access control flow with MFA, RBAC, and least privilege principles
examples.code:
ALGORITHM IAM_Access_Control
INPUT: user_id, resource_request, user_credentials, user_roles
OUTPUT: access_decision (ALLOW/DENY), session_token

BEGIN
 // Step 1: Authentication
 IF NOT Validate_Credentials(user_credentials) THEN
 RETURN DENY, "Invalid credentials"
 END IF

 // Step 2: Multi-Factor Authentication Check
 IF NOT Verify_MFA(user_id) THEN
 RETURN DENY, "MFA verification failed"
 END IF

 // Step 3: Get User Roles via RBAC
 user_roles ← Get_Roles_From_RBAC(user_id)

 // Step 4: Get Permissions for Roles
 required_permissions ← Get_Required_Permissions(resource_request)

 // Step 5: Check Principle of Least Privilege
 FOR EACH role IN user_roles DO
 role_permissions ← Get_Role_Permissions(role)
 IF Contains(role_permissions, required_permissions) THEN
 // Verify user has minimum necessary permissions
 granted_permissions ← Intersect(role_permissions, required_permissions)
 IF Size(granted_permissions) <= Size(required_permissions) THEN
 // Step 6: Just-In-Time Access Check
 IF Check_JIT_Eligibility(user_id, required_permissions) THEN
 session_token ← Generate_Temp_Session(user_id, granted_permissions)
 RETURN ALLOW, session_token
 END IF
 END IF
 END IF
 END FOR

 // Step 7: Log access denial for security monitoring
 Log_Access_Attempt(user_id, resource_request, DENY)
 RETURN DENY, "Insufficient permissions"
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates the IAM access control flow incorporating: 1) Credential validation, 2) MFA verification for strong authentication, 3) RBAC for role-based permission assignment, 4) Least privilege checking ensuring only necessary permissions are granted, 5) JIT access for temporary elevated privileges. This flow ensures defense-in-depth at the IAM layer.

examples.title: Python: Data Encryption Implementation
examples.description: Demonstrates encryption at rest and in transit using Python's cryptography libraries
examples.explanation: This Python implementation demonstrates key data protection strategies: 1) Data classification with different encryption levels, 2) AES-256 encryption at rest using PBKDF2 for key derivation, 3) TLS simulation for data in transit, 4) DLP pattern matching to detect sensitive data (credit cards, SSN, API keys). This aligns with defense-in-depth data protection layer and encryption everywhere best practice.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for DLP scanning where n is data length

examples.title: Python: Security Monitoring and SIEM Implementation
examples.description: Demonstrates SIEM-style log aggregation, threat detection, and automated alerting
examples.explanation: This implementation demonstrates security monitoring and incident response strategies: 1) SIEM system that aggregates events from multiple cloud sources (simulating CloudTrail, GuardDuty), 2) IDS/IPS threat detection using attack pattern matching and rule-based detection, 3) Automated alerting with severity levels and action recommendations, 4) Behavioral baseline building for anomaly detection, 5) Security reporting for compliance and forensics. This supports the monitoring and logging layer of defense-in-depth.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for event ingestion and alert processing
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) for storing events and alerts

examples.title: Python: IAM Policy Definition and Validation
examples.description: Demonstrates IAM policy configuration with RBAC, least privilege, and policy validation
examples.explanation: This implementation demonstrates core IAM concepts: 1) RBAC with predefined roles and permission management, 2) Least privilege policy validation that checks for wildcards and missing conditions, 3) IAM policy JSON generation compatible with AWS/Azure, 4) JIT access management for temporary elevated privileges, 5) Permission checking with wildcard support. This covers the IAM layer of defense-in-depth with MFA, RBAC, least privilege, and JIT access concepts.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for policy validation where n is number of statements
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) for storing roles and permissions
